title: A finite nonnegative row-stochastic row is infinity-norm stable

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: A finite nonnegative row whose weights sum to one maps any input vector bounded by M in absolute value to an output bounded by M.
informal.latex: a_i\ge 0,\ \sum_i a_i=1,\ |x_i|\le M \Rightarrow |\sum_i a_i x_i|\le M.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
a is a nonnegative real-valued row over s
the row weights sum to one over s
x is bounded by M in absolute value on s
informal.expected_conclusion: The finite row application is bounded in absolute value by M.

declarations.name: finiteRowApply
declarations.statement: def finiteRowApply {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x : ι → Real) : Real := ∑ i ∈ s, a i * x i

declarations.name: finiteRowApply_abs_le
declarations.statement: theorem finiteRowApply_abs_le {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x : ι → Real) (M : Real) (ha : ∀ i, i ∈ s → 0 <= a i) (hsum : ∑ i ∈ s, a i = 1) (hx : ∀ i, i ∈ s → |x i| <= M) : |finiteRowApply s a x| <= M

tasks.input: Translate finite row-stochastic infinity-norm stability into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem finiteRowApply_abs_le {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (a x : ι → Real) (M : Real) (ha : ∀ i, i ∈ s → 0 <= a i) (hsum : ∑ i ∈ s, a i = 1) (hx : ∀ i, i ∈ s → |x i| <= M) : |finiteRowApply s a x| <= M

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by applying the triangle inequality for finite sums, simplifying absolute values of nonnegative weights, using the pointwise bound, and rewriting the row sum.
tasks.target: Use Finset.abs_sum_le_sum_abs, abs_mul, abs_of_nonneg, Finset.sum_le_sum, Finset.sum_mul, and the row-sum identity.

tasks.input: Find the Mathlib lemma that bounds the absolute value of a finite sum by the sum of absolute values.
